content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to find the maximum and minimum elements in an array using pointers.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer NNN, denoting the number of elements in the array.
Second line of input contains NNN space separated integers, the elements of the array.


Output Format

Output on a single line, the max value in the array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of elements in the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the maximum value among the given N integers.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array is [3,4,1,2,3]; the largest element is 4.
Only one element, so the maximum equals that element.

solution.approach: We simply scan each array once while keeping the current maximum. Because each element is examined exactly once, the algorithm is optimal for this problem. Using BufferedReader and StringTokenizer ensures fast I/O for large inputs. For every test case we initialise max to the smallest possible integer, update it for each element, and output the final max.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringTokenizer st;int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){int N=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int max=Integer.MIN_VALUE;for(int i=0;i<N;i++){int val=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());if(val>max)max=val;}sb.append(max);if(t<T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
